title: Manually-curated timeline entries
description: Hand-edited in-world timeline entries that flow into the canonical data/derived/timeline.json on the next build. Each entry is atomic (one event); use this file for chapter-anchored events that aren't derivable from the xlsx, wiki, or TUI annotations. chapter_num here is the only place a human-vetted explicit chapter linkage can live (besides TUI spans).

id.description: Stable, human-chosen slug. Lowercase alphanumeric + hyphen + colon (for namespacing). Will be prefixed with 'manual:' in the canonical timeline.

in_world_date_iso.description: ISO date (day-precision). Entries dated before the in-narration cutoff defined in scripts/derive_timeline.py are dropped at merge time.

chapter_num.description: Optional explicit chapter linkage. Set only when you've verified it from the source text. Omit (or set to null) when uncertain.

time_of_day.description: Optional coarse diurnal anchor (morning / afternoon / evening / night). Set only when the source text attests it; otherwise omit or null.

note.description: Optional internal note for the curator (NOT propagated to the canonical timeline). Use this to record why an entry was added or what source you used.
